title: Variable internal combustion engine crank
abstract: The invention relates to a variable internal combustion engine crank, consisting of a piston (1) which moves inside a cylinder (2) which is connected to a connecting rod (3), forming a joint. The connecting rod (3) is connected to a crankshaft (5) via a connecting element (12). A pin (10) moves within a longitudinal hole (13) of the connecting element (12), the pin travelling in a predetermined embossed path (11). When compressing and expanding the piston (1), the pin (10) moves in the embossed path (11) changing the point of rotation and the distance from the bearing point of the connecting rod (3).
claims: Claims 1. Variable internal combustion engine crank, consisting of a piston (1), moving in a cylinder (2), where the piston (1) is firmly connected with a connecting rod (3) and the rod (3) by means of a connecting element (12) having a longitudinal hole (13), is connected to a crankshaft (5) via a pin (10) which simultaneously is driven into an embossed path (11), characterized in that the movement of the pin (10) in the embossed path (11) changes the point of rotation of the connecting rod (3). 2. A variable internal combustion engine crank according to claim 1, characterized in that it is fixedly connected to an adjacent variable crank through a spindle (14), transferring the movement of the common crankshaft (5) to the adjacent crank via gears (15).
